subject: Project Management
title: The Three Gorges Dam
description: A quick, practical guide to The Three Gorges Dam.

hook.text: China built the world's largest dam — so massive it slightly slowed the Earth's rotation.

buildup.text: Construction took 17 years, 40,000 workers, and 27 million cubic meters of concrete.

discovery.text: The dam generates 22,500 megawatts — as much power as 15 nuclear reactors.

twist.text: 1.3 million people were forcibly relocated. Entire cities were submerged under the reservoir.

climax.text: The Three Gorges Dam is a triumph of engineering and a tragedy of displacement — inseparable legacies.

punchline.text: The world's most powerful dam came at a human cost.

quiz.question: How many people were relocated for the Three Gorges Dam?
